NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITIONER'S PETITION FOR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE AND APPLICATION FOR CALENDAR PREFERENCE. FILED BY PETITIONER HERRERA, FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER FRANCISCO HERRERA'S PETITION FOR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE AND APPLICATION FOR CALENDAR PREFERENCE IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 14, 2016 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302 FOR FURTHER HEARING. COURT PRELIMINARILY FINDS THE MOTION WAS MADE IN HASTE AND IMPROPERLY SUPPORTED. THE COURT GRANTS BOTH SIDES LEAVE TO INVESTIGATE, BRIEF THE COURT AND PROVIDE ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE, IN WRITING AND ADDRESSING CERTAIN POINTS INCLUDING (1) WHAT ACTION CAN/SHOULD THE COURT TAKE, (2) WAS THERE AN ERROR MADE?, AND (3) IF AN ERROR WAS MADE, SHOULD THE PLAINTIFF RAISED IT EARLIER. REGARDING PETITIONER'S DECLARATION FILED SEPTEMBER 1, 2016, THE COURT REQURIES INFORMATION FROM A "QUALIFIED PERSON", OTHERWISE IT IS HEARSAY. PETITIONER HAS LEAVE UNTIL THE END OF BUSINESS ON SEPTEMBER 9, 2016 TO SUBMIT. RESPONDENT, THE CITY HAS UNTIL THE END OF BUSINESS ON SEPTEMBER 12, 2016 TO SUBMIT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR #59761, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER FRANCISCO HERRERA'S PETITION FOR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE AND APPLICATION FOR CALENDAR PREFERENCE IS DENIED (SEE ORDER FOR FULL TEXT). calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petitioner'S Petition For Alternative Writ Of Mandate And Application For Calendar Preference
Off Calendar Sep-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Francisco Herrera (415-812-9362) appears as petitioner, In Propria Persona

Deputy City Attorney, Andrew Shen of the City Attorney's Office (415-554-4780) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, John Arntz as director of City and County of San Francisco Department of Elections

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER FRANCISCO HERRERA'S Petition For Alternative Writ Of Mandate And Application For Calendar Preference is DENIED. Petitioner has failed to satisfy his burden that his requested relief will not substantially interfere with the process of printing and distributing the materials for the upcoming election nor has he shown that the Chinese transliteration of his name by respondent is erroneous. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Hearing On Petitioner'S Petition For Alternative Writ Of Mandate And Application For Calendar Preference
Off Calendar Sep-08-2016 Continued To Sep-14-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Francisco Herrera (415-812-9362) appears as petitioner, In Propria Persona

Deputy City Attorney, Joshua S. White (415-554-4661) appears as counsel for respondent, City and County of San Francisco Department of Elections

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court sets aside the tentative ruling for now.

PETITIONER FRANCISCO HERRERA'S Petition For Alternative Writ Of Mandate And Application For Calendar Preference is CONTINUED to September 14, 2016 at 9:30 AM in Department 302 for further hearing. Court preliminarily finds the motion was made in haste and improperly supported. The Court grants both sides leave to investigate, brief the Court and provide admissible evidence, in writing and addressing certain points including (1) What action can/should the Court take, (2) Was there an error made?, and (3) if an error was made, should the plaintiff raised it earlier. Regarding petitioner's declaration filed September 1, 2016, the Court requries information from a "qualified person", otherwise it is hearsay. Petitioner has leave until the end of business on September 9, 2016 to submit. Respondent, the City has until the end of business on September 12, 2016 to submit.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
